title: Fire safety: Specialised housing and care homes
body: "The research examines the range of specialised housing and care home typologies in England and whether Approved Document B (fire safety) provides adequate guidance on fire safety for each.    The researchers reviewed:     sector-specific guidance, considering its evidence base and effectiveness  existing buildings to understand how different design approaches can affect the fire strategy and the use of fire-engineered solutions.  international regulations to ascertain best practice from a selection of other countries  how mitigating provisions affect fire safety risk across specialised housing typologies the benefits of protected storage for mobility scooters   Background  The government commissioned the research as part of a technical review of Approved Document B (fire safety).    PRP Architects carried out the work, and the findings and recommendations do not represent government policy. "
